BICE COMPANY INC.'S MOTION TO DEEM REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL VERIFIED RESPONSES TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY FROM DEFENDANT JOHN K. BICE COMPANY, INC. AND MOTION TO DEEM REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION ADMITTED ARE DENIED. DEFENDANT, A DISSOLVED CORPORATION, IS UNDER NO OBLIGATION TO PROVIDE VERIFICATIONS. (MALDONADO V. SUPERIOR COURT (2002) 94 CAL.APP.4TH 1390.) SINCE DEFENDANT IS NOT REQUIRED TO PROVIDE VERIFICATIONS, DEFENDANT'S TIMELY ANSWERS TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION WILL NOT BE DEEMED ADMITTED. BOTH PLAINTIFF'S AND DEFENDANT'S REQUESTS FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: JOYCE D. FINLEY, CSR # 8620

Off Calendar Apr-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Young S. Lee of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Anthony Lima

RULING:
BERRY & BERRY AND FOR DEFENDANT HILL BROTHERS CHEMICAL COMPANY'S MOTION TO IMPOSE SANCTIONS AGAINST KELLER FISHBACK JACKSON UNDER CCP SECTION 128.7(B)(1) & (2)

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Berry & Berry's motion to impose sanctions of $5,240 against Keller Fishback & Jackson is denied. Berry & Berry has not demonstrated, pursuant to C.C.P. 128.7(b), that plaintiff's previous disqualification motion was brought for an improper or harassing purpose or that plaintiff's arguments were frivolous. Although Berry & Berry's memorandum of points and authorities mentions a request for additional sanctions payable to the court, such request was not properly included in the notice of motion, and thus is denied. (C.C.P. 2023.040.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

J. Bruce Jackson, Esq., of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Anthony Lima
Anthony Bentivegna, Esq., of BECHERER KANNETT & SCHWEITZER (510-658-3600), appearing for defendant / opposing party John K. Bice Company Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFF ANTHONY LIMA'S MOTION TO COMPEL VERIFIED DISCOVERY RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT JOHN K. BICE COMPANY INC.'S MOTION TO DEEM REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to compel verified responses to written discovery from defendant John K. Bice Company, Inc. and motion to deem requests for admission admitted are denied. Defendant, a dissolved corporation, is under no obligation to provide verifications. (Maldonado v. Superior Court (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1390.) Since defendant is not required to provide verifications, defendant's timely answers to plaintiff's requests for admission will not be deemed admitted. Both plaintiff's and defendant's requests for monetary sanctions are denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Joyce D. Finley, CSR # 8620 = (503/TLJ)

RULING:
Defendant The Sherwin-Williams Co.'s amended demurrer to plaintiff's amended complaint. After argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows: OVERRULE demurrer to the entirety of plaintiff's amended complaint. Defendant's demurrer to plaintiff's negligence and strict liability causes of action is overruled. The lengthy list of products does not in and of itself mean that the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich, 21 Cal. 4th 71 (1999) are not met, presuming plaintiff's counsel satisfies the requirements of CCP 128.7 (b). See extended discussion of CCP 128.7 in Bockrath, 21 Cal. 4th at 82-83. Prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order.
